title: Responsible Borrowing Checkpoint
outcomePassed: You can now recognise and avoid harmful borrowing patterns.
outcomeFailed: Review the scenarios to strengthen your borrowing decisions.
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you evaluate if a borrowing decision is truly necessary?
What steps should you take before agreeing to any loan?

stages.prompt: You are considering a loan for a luxury vacation. What is the most responsible approach?

options.label: Take the loan as vacations are important for mental health
options.reflection: While vacations can be important, borrowing for luxury vacations is generally not financially responsible.

options.label: Save up for the vacation instead of borrowing
options.reflection: Correct! Saving for discretionary expenses like vacations is more financially responsible than borrowing.

options.label: Take a loan but make it small
options.reflection: Any loan for luxury expenses is unnecessary debt that creates interest obligations.

options.label: Borrow using a credit card instead of a loan
options.reflection: Using credit cards for luxury expenses still creates unnecessary debt obligations.

stages.prompt: You're experiencing temporary financial stress. What should you do before considering a loan?

options.label: Review your budget and cut non-essential expenses first
options.reflection: Exactly! Before borrowing, examine your expenses and see if adjustments can solve the temporary stress.

options.label: Immediately apply for multiple loans to cover expenses
options.reflection: Applying for multiple loans during stress can lead to over-indebtedness and financial problems.

options.label: Borrow from friends instead of formal lenders
options.reflection: Borrowing from friends can strain relationships and doesn't solve the underlying financial issue.

options.label: Use all available credit lines immediately
options.reflection: Using all credit lines can create dangerous over-leverage and doesn't address the root cause.

stages.prompt: Your friend encourages you to take a loan for a business opportunity that seems lucrative. What do you do?

options.label: Trust your friend and take the loan immediately
options.reflection: Making financial decisions based solely on others' advice without your own analysis is risky.

options.label: Take half the loan amount to minimize risk
options.reflection: Even partial borrowing for unverified opportunities carries significant risk.

options.label: Borrow more than suggested to maximize potential profits
options.reflection: Increasing borrowed amounts amplifies potential losses if the opportunity doesn't work out.

options.label: Evaluate the business opportunity yourself before deciding
options.reflection: Right! Evaluate any business opportunity based on your own analysis of risks and returns.

stages.prompt: You qualify for a loan amount higher than what you need. What is the most responsible choice?

options.label: Take the full amount since you qualify
options.reflection: Taking more than you need increases your debt burden unnecessarily and creates higher interest costs.

options.label: Take slightly more to have a buffer
options.reflection: Taking more than needed still creates unnecessary debt obligations, even with a buffer rationale.

options.label: Take only the amount you actually need
options.reflection: Perfect! Borrowing only what you need minimizes your debt obligation and interest costs.

options.label: Negotiate to increase the approved amount
options.reflection: Increasing approved amounts when you don't need them increases unnecessary debt obligations.

stages.prompt: You're considering a loan with an EMI that takes up 60% of your income. What should you do?

options.label: Take the loan as long as you can make the payments
options.reflection: An EMI that takes 60% of income is extremely risky and leaves no room for other expenses or emergencies.

options.label: Look for a loan with lower EMI or reconsider the need
options.reflection: Correct! EMIs should ideally be below 30% of income to maintain financial stability.

options.label: Take the loan but reduce other expenses drastically
options.reflection: Reducing other expenses drastically to accommodate high EMIs is unsustainable and risky.

options.label: Take the loan and hope your income increases
options.reflection: Relying on future income increases to service high EMIs is risky and can lead to financial stress.
